subject: Hadith
title: ‫ِه‬ ‫ٱلَّل َع َل ى ٱۡل ۡؤ ِم ِنی ِإ ۡذ َث ِفی‬ ‫َق‬ ‫َل‬‫‫‬
text:
‫ِه‬ ‫ٱلَّل َع َل ى ٱۡل ۡؤ ِم ِنی ِإ ۡذ َث ِفی‬ ‫َق‬ ‫َل‬‫‫‬
‫ۡم‬ ‫َن َبَع‬ ‫ُم‬ ‫ۡد َم َّن ُه‬
‫واࣰل ِّم َأ نُف ِس ِه َیۡتُل و۟ا َع َل ِه اَیٰـِتِهۦ‬
‫ۡی ۡم َء‬ ‫ۡم‬ ‫ۡن‬ ‫َر ُس‬
‫َو ُیَز ِّكیِه ۡم َو ُیَع ِّل ُم ُه ُم ٱۡلِك َتٰـَب َو ٱۡلِح ۡك َم َة َو ِإ ن اَك ُن ۟او‬
‬﴾ ١٦٤ ﴿ ‫ِم َق ۡبُل َلِف ی َض َل ٰـࣲل ُّم ِبیٍن‬
‫ن‬
Indeed, Allah has done the believers a ˹great˺ favour by raising a messenger from among them—
reciting to them His revelations, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom. For indeed
they had previously been clearly astray. (3 - 164)

subject: Hadith
title: Grave warning on misquoting or lying about the
text:
Grave warning on misquoting or lying about the
Hadith of the Prophet (SAW)
The Prophet (SAW) said, “Whoever tells a lie about me intentionally, then let
him take his seat in the fire.” (Al Tirmidhi)
The Prophet (SAW) said, “Ascribing false things to me is not like
ascribing false things to anyone else. Whosoever tells a lie against me
intentionally then surely let him occupy his seat in Hellfire.”
(Al Bukhari)

religion. If it was not for the isnād then anyone could have said
whatever they wanted to say.”

decided to sit in a gathering of Hudhaifah RA with
other Muslims.

subject: Hadith

text:
Different forms of Dhulm (oppression)
The worst form of oppression is shirk (associating partners
with Allah)
Oppressing yourself (persisting in sin, neglecting
obligations)
Oppressing others (physical, social, emotional, financial)

subject: Hadith
title: Discussion point
text:
Discussion point
Have we lost the essence of Ramadan?
The hunger we experience is meant to aid us in abstaining
from sin, not just our diet.
We don’t anticipate the worship, rather we start preparing
for iftaar dawats, trying new recipes, and taraweeh
becomes a time where socializing is the primary objective.
Suhoor Fests and the marketization of Ramadan

subject: Hadith
title: Hadith 8
text:
Hadith 8
‫ا‬
‫َي ُم ُج َّنٌة‬ ‫ِّص‬‫ال‬
“Fasting is a shield”

subject: Hadith
title: The purpose of hunger and Fasting
text:
The purpose of hunger and Fasting
Fasting is a prescription, prescribed to us by our creator
Hunger builds self-restraint and disciplne
Hunger tames the nafs
Fasting teaches patience and delayed gratification
Fasting is a shield from sin and evil forces
The reward for fasting is unquantifiable

subject: Hadith
title: Make this Ramadan unforgettable
text:
Make this Ramadan unforgettable
Ramadan is a building block
Make a realistic schedule that you can follow
Start preparing from now
Attend Taraweeh every night and praying salah in congregation
Recite a portion of Quran daily (aim for a khatam)
Daily adhkaar
Aim to finish a book of Seerah during this month

subject: Hadith
title: Consistency over Intensity
text:
Consistency over Intensity
Consistency builds habits. Habits define who you really
are.
Consistency in deeds brings delayed gratification
Intense sessions of an action will bring instant gratification
and a false sense of accomplishment

subject: Hadith

text:

Ramadan is the perfect example of this comparison
Majority of people do not maintain the good habits they’ve
built during this month
Being consistent

subject: Hadith

Build towards achieving something this Ramadan
The opportunity to bring change to your habits,
perspective of life, and your imaan is now

‫َب ُه‬ ‫ْن‬ ‫َذ‬ ‫اَل‬ ‫َم‬ ‫َك‬ ‫ِب‬ ‫ْن‬ ‫َّذ‬‫ل‬‫ا‬ ‫ِم‬ ‫َا لَّتاِئ‬
‫ْن‬ ‫ن‬ ‫ُب‬
“The one who repents from sin, is
like the one who has no sin.”

subject: Hadith
title: What is Tawbah?
text:
What is Tawbah?
Tawbah is to repent. Specifically repentance from major
sins.
Minor deeds can be forgiven via many deeds. Tawbah is
necessary to have your major sins forgiven.

subject: Hadith
title: The conditions for a successful Tawbah
text:
The conditions for a successful Tawbah
Being sincere to Allah with your tawbah
To recognize that you have committed a grave sin
You must have a feeling of regret over the action committed
You must have a strong resolve that you will never return to this
action again

text:
Hadith 10
‫ِإ ْيَم انََا َأ ْح َس ُن ُه ْم ُخ ُل ق ََا‬ ‫َا ْك َم ُل َا ْلُم ْؤ ِم ِن‬
‫َن‬ ‫ْي‬
“The most perfect of believers is
the one with the best of manners.”

subject: Hadith
title: Levels of Imaan
text:
Levels of Imaan
What is Imaan?
Imaan is intangible
A person can have deficiencies in their imaan

subject: Hadith
title: Is being a good person sufficient for salvation?
text: Is being a good person sufficient for salvation?

subject: Hadith
title: Narrator of this hadith
text:

Abu Malik Al Harith Al Ash’ari

subject: Hadith
title: Hadith 11
text:
Hadith 11
‫َا ُّط ُه ْو ُر َش ْط ُر اِاْل ْيَم اِن‬
“Purification is half of faith.”

subject: Hadith
title: True purification
text:
True purification
The objective is to achieve inner purification
Inner purification can only be achieved once the exterior is
clean and pure
Every act of worship is linked to being in a pure state

subject: Hadith
title: Exterior purity will lead to inner purity
text:
Exterior purity will lead to inner purity
Knowing the rules of Taharah thoroughly
Being very cautious when using the bathroom
Many times our OCD comes from shaytaan.
When achieving purification, be diligent in your method and
avoid waswasa from Shaytaan

yourselves.”

title: Why is saying salaam important
text:
Why is saying salaam important
Cultivating brotherhood/sisterhood
It is a dua you make for the person you meet
Giving Salaams to everyone is a way to remove pride from
ones heart

text:
Hadith 13
‫ْب‬ ‫َض‬ ‫َتْغ‬ ‫اَل‬
“Do not be angry.”

subject: Hadith
title: The dangers of anger
text:
The dangers of anger
It can lead a person to say or do something they will regret
It can lead you to making a decision which is oppressive
The remedy
Saying istiaadha
Changing your position
Make Wudhu

Hadith 14

“Allah is gentle and loves the
quality of gentleness.”

subject: Hadith
title: Being gentle in every aspect of life
text:
Being gentle in every aspect of life
Gentleness is a trait that needs to be consciously applied
This quality is not only restricted to human beings but
should be applied in everything
The one who is deprived of rifq is deprived of all good

title: Narrator of this hadith

Abu Hurairah RA

Hadith 15

‫ْي‬ ‫ْي ْن‬
“The fortunate one is the one who
takes advice from others.”

subject: Hadith
title: Learning from the experiences of others
text:
Learning from the experiences of others
We can learn from the experiences/life lessons of others
We may not be able to bear the same trials others go
through
If a persons heeds the advice he/she is given, they may be
able to avoid an outcome they may not have expected

text:
Hadith 16
‫ا‬ ‫ِع‬
‫َا لُّد َع اُء ُه َو َا ْل َب َد ُة‬
“Dua is worship.”

subject: Hadith
title: What happens when a person makes dua?
text:
What happens when a person makes dua?
Not every dua is accepted
There are 3 outcomes when a person makes dua
The dua must be made sincerely
Your dua should not involve sin
Do not be hasty when making dua

subject: Hadith
title: Method of making Dua
text:
Method of making Dua
Cup your hands
Place them near your chest
Begin your dua with Salawaat
Begin your dua with praises of Allah
Before you make dua for yourself, make dua for your
parents and others
End your dua by sending salutations on rasulullah saw
Wipe over your face

subject: Hadith
title: Hadith 17
text:
Hadith 17
‫ﻌ‬ َ َ
‫َ ﮴ ﮲ّ َ ُه‬‫و‬ ،‫ﺎ‬ َ ‫﮲‬ ‫ﺎ‬ َ ‫﮲‬ ‫ﻛ‬
َ ‫ق‬ َ ‫ز‬
ِ ‫ر‬ ‫و‬َ ‫ﻢ‬
ُ َ ْ ْ‫﮲‬‫ﻠ‬َ ‫ﺳ‬ ‫ا‬ َ ‫ﮟ‬‫ﻣ‬َ ‫ﺢ‬
َ ‫ﻠ‬
َ ْ ‫﮲‬ ‫ا‬َ ‫ﺪ‬ْ َ ‫﮴‬
‫ا‬ ‫ٓا‬ ‫ا‬
‫ُه َم َت ُه‬ ‫ِب‬ ‫ّٰل‬‫ل‬ ‫َا‬
“Indeed that person is successful, who accepts Islam and
is provided sufficient sustenance, and whom Allah makes
content with what He has given him.”

subject: Hadith
title: What is Rizq (Sustenance)?
text:
What is Rizq (Sustenance)?
The meaning of Rizq is not only confined to sustenance
Rizq can reach a person in many ways; wealth, health,
blessings etc.
Although a persons Rizq has been written, how you
achieve it determines on the method you choose.
A persons Rizq has been determined but he/she still needs
to strive in achieving it

subject: Hadith
title: ‫‪Achieving Rizq‬‬
text:
‫‪Achieving Rizq‬‬
‫اﻟﺴ ْ﮵ َﻄﺎن ﮵َ ِﻌ ُﺪ ُﻛ ُﻢ ا ْﻟ َ﮲ ﮴ْ َﺮ َو ﮵َ ْٔ ُﻣ ُﺮ ُﻛﻢ ِ﮳ﺎ ْﻟ َ﮲ ْﺤ ﮶َﺴ ِﺎء ۖ َواﻟ َّﻠ ُﻪ ﮵َ ِﻌ ُﺪ ُﻛﻢ‬ ‫َ‬ ‫ّ‬ ‫﮶‬
‫ُ‬
‫َّﻣ ْ﮲ﻌ ﮲ ِ َﺮ ًة ّ ِﻣ ْ﮲ ُﻪ َو َ﮲ ْ﮲ﺼ ًﻼ ۗ َواﻟ َّﻠ ُﻪ َو ِاﺳ ٌﻊ َﻋ ِﻠ ﮵ ٌﻢ‬
‫)‪(Surah Al-Baqarah, 2:268‬‬

text:
Hadith 18
، ‫الَّناَس‬ ‫ِم‬ ‫ِنْع َم َتاِن َم ْغ ْو ِف ِه َم ا َك ِث‬
‫ْيٌر َن‬ ‫ُب ٌن ْي‬
ِّ
‫اﻟﺼ َﺤ ُﺔ و ا ْﻟ ﮲َ َﺮ ِغا‬
“Many are decieved regarding two favors; good health and
free time.”

subject: Hadith
title: The race against time and health
text:
The race against time and health
A race with an unknown finish line
Imagine time being the track you are running on and
health being your ability to run
A strong and healthy body without iman is wasted. A sick
and deprived body with iman struggles.

subject: Hadith
title: The trade off
text:
The trade off
Your life is being exchanged for time
And with time, your health is deteriorating
How to become successful in this trade
Understand the value of what you are exchaning

Immediately after the worship of Allah, dealing with your
parents with ihsaan (excellence) is mentioned.
What does ihsaan mean? And what is the definition in this
context?

Hadith 20
‫ِا َّ ُ ﻮ اﻟﻠّٰ ﻪ ﮲ ِ ﮵ﻰ ﻫ ِﺬ ِه ا ْﻟ ﮳ﻬﺎ ِ ِﻢ ا ْﻟﻤﻌ ﮳ﺤﻤِة‬
َ َ ْ ُ ٔ ََ َ ْ َ ْ ‫﮴﮴‬
“Fear Allah in regard to these animals that cannot speak.”

subject: Hadith
title: Treating animals with ihsaan
text:
Treating animals with ihsaan
A person must be conscious of Allah in all aspects of life.
Allah SWT has made animals subservient to human beings.
That does not give us the license to be abusive.
The prophet SAW rendered many warnings against those
who show cruelty against animals. Even in acts such as
slaughter, we are to show up most care and efficiency.

subject: Hadith
title: Treating animals with ihsaan (continued)
text:
Treating animals with ihsaan (continued)
The sunnah method of slaughter
2 incidents narrated by the prophet SAW in regards to the
good treatment and mistreatment of animals.

‫َ﮲ ِﺎﻋ ِﻠِه‬
“Whoever guides someone to good will have a reward
equal to that of the one who does it.”

subject: Hadith
title: Narrator of this hadith
text:
Narrator of this hadith
Uthman ibn Affan RA

subject: Hadith
title: Hadith 36
text:
Hadith 36
‫ﻤ‬ َ
‫ﻠ‬ ّ ‫ﻋ‬
َ ‫و‬ ‫ن‬‫أ‬ٓ ‫ﺮ‬ ُ ‫ﻟ‬
ْ ‫ا‬ ‫ﻢ‬‫ﻠ‬َ ّ ‫ﻌ‬َ ‫ﮟ‬‫ﻣ‬ ‫ﻢ‬‫ﻛ‬ُ ‫ﺮ‬ ‫﮵‬ ‫ﺣ‬
َ
‫﮲ ْ ُ ْ َ ْ﮲ ﮴ َ َ ﮴ ْ َ َ َ ُه‬
“The best among you are those who learn the Quran and
teach it.”

subject: Hadith
title: Assignment
text:
Assignment
